title: ActionBar & FilterBar
intro: Two horizontal bars sit above a grid and they do opposite jobs. `wf-action-bar` carries verbs — the actions that operate on the collection or a selection. `wf-filter-bar` carries predicates — the controls that narrow what's shown. When a single record needs a single action, neither bar is the answer: drop an inline button next to it.

blocks.body:
The toolbar decision is a verb-vs-noun split (DESIGN.md 4.3). If the control changes *what the user can do* to the data, it belongs in an `wf-action-bar`. If it changes *what data is shown*, it belongs in a `wf-filter-bar`. If it touches exactly one record, it's an inline button living next to that record — not a bar at all.

`wf-action-bar` is a three-zone flexbox: `wf-action-bar-left` (title + count), an optional `wf-action-bar-center`, and `wf-action-bar-right` (the buttons). It space-betweens its zones, sits on a white surface with a `--wf-color-border` outline, and is where bulk verbs — Export, Bulk Edit, Delete — live. Add `wf-action-bar-selection` when rows are checked: the bar flips to a blue `#eff6ff` fill with a `--wf-color-primary` border so the selection context is unmistakable.

`wf-filter-bar` is the query surface. It groups `wf-filter-item` controls (a `wf-filter-label` + a `wf-select`/`wf-input`) on the left and keeps its own quiet actions — Clear All, Apply — pinned right in `wf-filter-bar-actions`. It reads on a muted `--wf-color-neutral-50` ground so it recedes behind the grid it governs. Applied filters echo below as dismissible `wf-active-filter` pills.

blocks.title: ActionBar — Selection Mode
blocks.caption: wf-action-bar-selection flips the bar to a blue eff6ff fill with a primary border. The left zone swaps the title for a checkbox + '3 selected' count; the right zone surfaces contextual Bulk Edit and a danger-flagged Delete.

blocks.title: ActionBar — Title + Inline Filters
blocks.caption: The full three-zone layout: title and count on the left, a lightweight wf-action-bar-filters group centered, and Export + primary Add New pinned right.

blocks.title: ActionBar structure & variants
blocks.intro: wf-action-bar is the wrapper; the three zone classes are the layout contract. Modifier classes stack on the wrapper.

rows.name: wf-action-bar
rows.when: The default bar above a grid or list. Three zones, space-between, white surface with a 1px border and 8px radius. Holds verbs only.
rows.code:
Contracts

24 items

rows.name: wf-action-bar-selection
rows.when: Rows are selected. Repaints the bar #eff6ff with a --wf-color-primary border; left zone shows a checkbox + selected count, right zone shows bulk verbs.
rows.code: 3 selected

rows.name: wf-action-bar-center
rows.when: You need lightweight inline controls (a status select, a search box) between the title and the actions. Wrap them in wf-action-bar-filters. This is NOT a substitute for a real FilterBar.

rows.name: wf-action-bar-compact
rows.when: Tighter layouts — drops padding from 12px 16px to 8px 12px.

rows.name: wf-action-bar-borderless
rows.when: The bar sits flush above table content — drops the full border and radius, keeping only a 1px bottom divider.

rows.name: wf-action-bar-sticky
rows.when: The bar must stay visible while the grid scrolls. Adds position: sticky; top: 0; z-index: 10.

blocks.title: FilterBar — Multiple Filters
blocks.caption: wf-filter-bar on its muted neutral-50 ground. Each wf-filter-item pairs a wf-filter-label with a wf-select; Reset + Apply Filters stay pinned right in wf-filter-bar-actions.

blocks.title: FilterBar structure & variants
blocks.intro: wf-filter-bar holds a wf-filter-bar-filters group (the predicates) and a wf-filter-bar-actions group (Clear All, Apply). Each predicate is a wf-filter-item.

rows.name: wf-filter-bar
rows.when: The default query bar above a grid. Muted --wf-color-neutral-50 ground, 1px border, 8px radius; filters left, actions right.

rows.name: wf-filter-item
rows.when: One predicate: a wf-filter-label paired with a control. A bare span inside it (e.g. 'to' between two dates) renders as tertiary-gray helper text.
rows.code: to

rows.name: wf-filter-bar-inline
rows.when: A minimal filter bar with no chrome — drops the background, border, and radius and tightens padding to 8px 12px. For 'Show / Sort by' style row controls.

rows.name: wf-filter-bar-vertical
rows.when: Narrow columns or filter sidebars — stacks filters in a column and space-betweens each label/control pair.

blocks.title: FilterBar — Active Filter Pills
blocks.caption: Applied filters echo below the bar as wf-active-filter pills, each with a wf-active-filter-remove dismiss button, closed by a wf-clear-filters text button. Keeps the active query state visible (DESIGN.md 4.3 toolbar choice).

blocks.title: Active filter pills
blocks.intro: wf-active-filters is a standalone wrapping row that surfaces the applied query. It can sit directly under a wf-filter-bar or live on its own.

rows.name: wf-active-filters
rows.when: The wrapping container for applied-filter pills. Wraps to multiple lines; commonly closed by a wf-clear-filters button.
rows.code: Status: Active Type: Supply

rows.name: wf-active-filter
rows.when: One applied filter as a rounded blue pill (#eff6ff fill, #bfdbfe border, primary text). Asymmetric padding leaves room for the remove button on the right.

rows.name: wf-active-filter-remove
rows.when: The dismiss control inside a pill. An 18px round button; on hover it inverts to a solid --wf-color-primary fill with white glyph.

rows.name: wf-clear-filters
rows.when: The 'reset everything' affordance at the end of the pill row. A borderless text button; often carries a count, e.g. 'Clear All (4)'.

blocks.title: Bar surfaces & accents
blocks.intro: The two bars are differentiated by their ground color, and the selection/pill blues are literal hexes baked into the CSS rather than tokens — match them exactly.

rows.use: wf-action-bar resting surface — white, so the verb bar reads as foreground.
wf-filter-bar ground — muted, so the query bar recedes behind the grid.
Selection-mode fill (wf-action-bar-selection) AND the wf-active-filter pill fill.
Selection-mode border, selected-count text, active-filter pill text, and the remove-button glyph.
wf-active-filter pill border — a lighter blue than the primary.
Default outline on both bars; also the 1px ActionBar divider span between button groups.
Bare helper spans inside a wf-filter-item (e.g. the 'to' between date inputs).
Pill rounding on wf-action-bar-count and wf-active-filter.

blocks.title: FilterBar above, ActionBar below
blocks.code:
Contracts

24 items
blocks.note: When a screen needs both filtering and acting, stack the two bars — never merge them into one (DESIGN.md 4.3 / anti-pattern 7.5).

blocks.title: Toolbar choice (DESIGN.md 4.3)
blocks.intro: The split is verbs vs. nouns vs. single records. Get the job right and the right bar follows.

items.rule: Verbs that operate on a collection or selection go in wf-action-bar.
items.why: Export, Bulk Edit, Delete Selected act on the whole list — the ActionBar's job is to verb.

items.rule: Predicates that narrow what's shown go in wf-filter-bar.
items.why: Search, dropdown filters, date ranges, saved views are nouns and predicates — the FilterBar's job. It does not contain action buttons (Clear All / Apply only reset and commit the query, they don't act on the data).

items.rule: A single action on a single record is an inline button, not a bar.
items.why: An 'Edit' in a row or 'Add member' next to a heading is scoped and non-bulk; a bar would imply it operates on the collection.

items.rule: Never crowd filtering and acting into one bar. Stack a FilterBar and an ActionBar instead.
items.why: Mixing verbs and predicates means the user can no longer tell what each control does (anti-pattern 7.5).

items.rule: Show the active query as wf-active-filter pills below the bar.
items.why: Dismissible pills keep the applied filters visible and individually removable; always pair with wf-clear-filters so there's a one-shot reset.

items.do: FilterBar on top, ActionBar below (or vice versa).
items.dont: One bar with both dropdown filters and an Export button.
items.why: Verbs and predicates do different jobs; combining them makes every control ambiguous (DESIGN.md 4.3, anti-pattern 7.5).

items.dont: A red Delete sitting in a wf-filter-bar
items.why: Destructive bulk verbs belong to the ActionBar's selection state; the FilterBar carries no action buttons.

items.do: Inline 'Edit' button in the row it edits.
items.dont: An ActionBar above a list whose only button edits one specific record.
items.why: A bar reads as operating on the collection; a single-record action should sit next to its record.

items.do: wf-action-bar-selection (blue fill + primary border) when rows are checked.
items.dont: Leaving the default white bar and silently swapping the buttons.
items.why: The color shift is the signal that the bar's actions now target the selection, not the whole list.

items.title: ActionBar-center is not a FilterBar
items.detail: wf-action-bar-center + wf-action-bar-filters lets you drop a lightweight select or search into the verb bar, but it's for one or two convenience controls only. Real filtering — multiple predicates, Clear/Apply, active pills — is a dedicated wf-filter-bar. Don't grow the center zone into a filter surface (DESIGN.md 4.3).

items.title: The selection blue is a literal hex, not a token
items.detail: wf-action-bar-selection and wf-active-filter both fill with #eff6ff hardcoded in specialized.css — there's no --wf token for it. The pill border #bfdbfe is likewise literal. Match these hexes exactly; don't substitute --wf-color-primary-dim.

items.title: FilterBar and ActionBar live on different grounds
items.detail: wf-action-bar is white (#ffffff); wf-filter-bar is --wf-color-neutral-50 (#f9fafb). The muted ground is deliberate — the query bar should recede behind the grid it governs while the verb bar reads as foreground. Don't repaint them to match.

items.title: wf-filter-bar-filters wraps; the actions don't
items.detail: The filters group has flex-wrap: wrap and will spill onto a second line as you add predicates, while wf-filter-bar-actions stays flex-shrink: 0 pinned right. Plan for the filter row growing taller, not the actions moving.

items.title: The ActionBar button divider is a bare span, not a class
items.detail: To separate button groups in wf-action-bar-right, the markup uses an inline-styled 1px-wide span (height 24px, --wf-color-border background) — there's no wf-action-bar-divider class. The wf-toolbar-divider class exists but belongs to the separate wf-toolbar component.
